message:
fix(fuzz): fuzz_socket MarkStale must poison fd to -1 to survive fd reuse

60s sweep on fuzz_socket caught a real harness bug: scenario {Pair,
MarkStale slot=255, Socket, Shutdown slot=157, ...} triggered
"shutdown on stale fd must fail / left: 0 / right: -1".

Root cause: MarkStale closes the slot's fd but leaves s.fd holding
the just-closed fd number. The kernel reuses freed fd numbers
aggressively — the next Socket() op may bind a new socket to the
same fd integer. The stale slot's stored fd then refers to a now-
valid different socket; shutdown(stale_slot.fd, ...) succeeds
because the OS sees a valid fd, breaking the harness's invariant
that "shutdown on stale fd must fail".

Set s.fd = -1 after close so subsequent ops on the stale slot
target the kernel's invalid-fd sentinel (which always fails). Same
spirit as fuzz_xdr 9aa402c0 — match the harness's invariants to
real OS behavior, not the harness's idealized state model.

Verified: 60s re-fuzz after the fix → 17,593 runs, 0 crashes (was
hitting the assertion repeatedly within ~120k runs).
